{{Quote|What in the three rings of Anstares is goin' on and who is that …|A smuggler, upon being contacted by Woetar|<ref name="Date">The [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventures published in ''[[The Politics of Contraband]]'', including "[[Free Time]]," are set in the [[Time/Legends|time]] period between the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and the events of ''[[Heir to the Empire]]''. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the former to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], and ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' sets the latter in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]].</ref>}}
'''Anstares VI''', also known as '''Anstares''', was a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] located in the [[Juvex sector/Legends|Juvex sector]]. It was [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] by three [[Planetary ring/Legends|planetary rings]] and eight [[Moon/Legends|moons]], the latter of which were a popular destination for visitors from both the [[Senex-Juvex]] region and the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]].

==Description==
Anstares VI,<ref name="TEA" /> also known simply as Anstares,<ref name="Politics">{{WEGCite|story=Free Time|book=The Politics of Contraband}}</ref> was a [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]]–type [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Anstares system/Legends|Anstares system]] of the [[Juvex sector/Legends|Juvex sector]], a part of the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]] region in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. The [[Senex-Juvex Loop]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] linked it to the [[Deminol system/Legends|Deminol]] and [[Zaria system/Legends|Zaria systems]].<ref name="TEA" />

Within its [[Star system/Legends|star system]], the planet occupied the sixth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position, and was itself encircled by three [[Planetary ring/Legends|planetary rings]] and orbited by eight habitable [[Moon/Legends|moons]]. Anstares' natural [[Satellite/Legends|satellites]] overlooked the planet's rings as well as the nearby [[Thull's Shroud/Legends|Thull's Shroud]] [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
At some point between [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] and [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]],<ref name="Date" /> when the [[Zarian]] [[Con artist/Legends|con artist]] [[Woetar]] unexpectedly contacted the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] of a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] duo on the<ref name="Politics" /> [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]]<ref name="TEA" /> planet [[Antiquity]], one of the smugglers mentioned the rings of Anstares in an expression of confusion.<ref name="Politics" />

==Inhabitants==
Like the rest of the Senex-Juvex worlds, Anstares fell under the control of the region's [[Noble/Legends|aristocratic]] [[Ancient Houses]]. The planet's moons were a popular destination for nobles of the Senex-Juvex as well as individuals from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and affluent tourists from the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Behind the scenes==
Anstares was introduced in "[[Free Time]]," a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure written by [[Gary Haynes]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in the [[1992]] supplement ''[[The Politics of Contraband]]''.<ref name="Politics" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' provided the planet's full name and placed it in grid square L-17.<ref name="TEA" />
